content:
First write $r=[r]+ \{r\}=n+f$ with $n\in\{0,1,2,\dots\}$ and $0\le f<1$.  The equation is
\[
25\,\{r\}+ [r]\;=\;25f+n\;=\;125.
\]
Hence 
\[
f=\frac{125-n}{25},
\]
and we need $0\le f<1$, i.e. 
\[
0\le\frac{125-n}{25}<1
\;\Longrightarrow\;
0\le125-n<25
\;\Longrightarrow\;
100<n\le125.
\]
So $n=101,102,\dots,125$, and for each
\[
r=n+f
\;=\;n+\frac{125-n}{25}
\;=\;\frac{24n+125}{25}.
\]
There are $125-101+1=25$ values of $n$.  Their sum is
\[
\sum_{n=101}^{125}r
=\sum_{n=101}^{125}\frac{24n+125}{25}
=\frac1{25}\Bigl(24\sum_{n=101}^{125}n+125\cdot25\Bigr).
\]
But 
\[
\sum_{n=101}^{125}n=\frac{(101+125)\cdot25}{2}=2825,
\]
so
\[
24\cdot2825+125\cdot25=67800+3125=70925,
\]
and dividing by $25$ gives $70925/25=2837$.  Hence the sum of all solutions is
\[
\boxed{2837}.
\]

First write $r=[r]+ \{r\}=n+f$ with $n\in\{0,1,2,\dots\}$ and $0\le f<1$.  The equation is

\]
Hence 
\[
f=\frac{125-n}{25},
\]
and we need $0\le f<1$, i.e. 
\[
0\le\frac{125-n}{25}<1
\;\Longrightarrow\;
0\le125-n<25
\;\Longrightarrow\;
100<n\le125.
\]
So $n=101,102,\dots,125$, and for each
\[
r=n+f
\;=\;n+\frac{125-n}{25}
\;=\;\frac{24n+125}{25}.
\]
There are $125-101+1=25$ values of $n$.  Their sum is
\[
\sum_{n=101}^{125}r
=\sum_{n=101}^{125}\frac{24n+125}{25}
=\frac1{25}\Bigl(24\sum_{n=101}^{125}n+125\cdot25\Bigr).
\]

\[
\sum_{n=101}^{125}n=\frac{(101+125)\cdot25}{2}=2825,
\]

\[
24\cdot2825+125\cdot25=67800+3125=70925,
\]
and dividing by $25$ gives $70925/25=2837$.  Hence the sum of all solutions is
\[
\boxed{2837}.
\]
